goose.bubble_style: Full-width pill: bg-text-primary (near white) text-background-primary (dark). Inverted color scheme — this is the most visually distinctive approach among all four tools.
goose.typography: Inherits from MarkdownContent with !text-inherit to override all markdown colors back to bubble foreground. Effectively same font size as assistant messages.
goose.timestamp: font-mono text-xs text-text-secondary, positioned below bubble. On hover: slides up -translate-y-4 + opacity-0 (reveal edit/copy actions beneath). Duration 200ms.
goose.hover_actions: Edit + Copy buttons revealed on hover, slide up from beneath timestamp slot. Clean opacity + translate transition.
goose.edit_mode: Full-width centered edit box overlaying the bubble position. max-w-4xl, border rounded-xl, shadow-lg. Two action buttons: 'Edit in Place' (secondary) + 'Fork Session' (primary). Informational helper text shown.
goose.accessibility: Edit button has aria-label with truncated message text, aria-expanded state tracked, keyboard: Escape=cancel, Cmd+Enter=save. Focus ring on edit button.

opencode.bubble_style: background: var(--surface-base), border: 1px solid var(--border-weak-base). Subtle contained box — NOT an inverted pill. Padding: 8px 12px. Very low visual weight.
opencode.alignment: Right-aligned, max-width: min(82%, 64ch) — capped at 64 chars wide
opencode.typography: font-size: var(--font-size-base), color: var(--text-strong), line-height: var(--line-height-large), weight: regular
opencode.hover_actions: Copy wrapper: opacity 0 by default, transitions to opacity 1 on container :hover/:focus-within. Very subtle.
opencode.timestamp: Inline with copy wrapper, text-align right, truncated. Part of user-message-copy-wrapper slot.
opencode.accessibility: data-interrupted attribute changes color to var(--text-weak) for interrupted messages. Container-level hover for actions.

cline.bubble_style: backgroundColor: var(--vscode-badge-background), text-badge-foreground. Uses VS Code theme variables directly. padding: p-2.5 pr-1. rounded-xs (near-square corners, very minimal).
cline.shape: rounded-xs — almost no rounding. Very utilitarian.
cline.alignment: Full width (no max-width cap), not right-aligned — left-aligned like assistant messages
cline.typography: text-sm (14px), whiteSpace: pre-line, wordWrap: break-word. highlightText() utility renders @mentions and file references with special colors.
cline.hover_states: None on base bubble. Edit mode triggered by click on bubble anywhere.
cline.edit_mode: Auto-resizing textarea (react-textarea-autosize). VS Code input background/foreground/border variables. Two action buttons: 'Restore All' + 'Restore Chat' (linked to checkpoint system).
cline.accessibility: No explicit aria labels on the bubble. Edit textarea auto-selects all on focus.
cline.notes: Cline does NOT right-align user messages. They sit in the same left-flow as assistant content — very different from all other tools. Very utilitarian VS Code extension aesthetic.

ava.bubble_style: bg-[var(--chat-user-bg)] text-[var(--chat-user-text)], shadow-[var(--shadow-sm)]. Uses semantic chat tokens. rounded-[var(--radius-2xl)] rounded-br-[var(--radius-sm)] — asymmetric corners (chat bubble shape).
ava.shape: rounded-2xl on 3 corners, radius-sm on bottom-right — classic chat bubble tail effect
ava.typography:
Plain text via MarkdownContent with messageRole='user' (renders as

, no markdown parsing). font-size: var(--chat-font-size), whitespace-pre-wrap.
ava.timestamp: 10px mono, tracking-wide, gray-6. On hover: group-hover:-translate-y-3 group-hover:opacity-0 (timestamp hides, actions reveal). Duration 200ms.
ava.hover_actions: MessageActions revealed on hover via absolute positioning.
ava.notes: The asymmetric corner radius (2xl/sm) is the most sophisticated of the four — it creates a true chat bubble tail. Goose uses uniform rounding, OpenCode uses minimal rounding, Cline ignores rounding entirely.

components_compared.gap: AVA's chat-user-bg and chat-user-text tokens are not defined in the token file read — they are likely theme-specific and may not be consistent across all 29 themes. Goose uses an inverted color scheme (text-primary as BG) for maximum bubble contrast — AVA's bubble may be too low-contrast in some themes. OpenCode's 64ch max-width prevents very long user messages from dominating the viewport — AVA lacks this cap. Cline's @mention highlighting in user messages is absent in AVA.
components_compared.fix: 1. Add explicit --chat-user-bg and --chat-user-text values in tokens.css for each theme to ensure contrast. Default: --chat-user-bg: var(--gray-4); --chat-user-text: var(--gray-12). 2. Cap user bubble width at min(85%, 68ch) to prevent line-length overrun. 3. Consider raising contrast of user bubble by using a slightly lighter background (var(--gray-5)) to better distinguish from the page background. 4. The asymmetric radius is good — keep it. 5. Add opacity-based timestamp reveal with actions (already has it — verify it works with the TimestampLine component).

goose.layout: w-[90%] left-aligned. No background or border — plain flow content. The message is NOT in a bubble.
goose.thinking_integration: Thinking rendered above main text as a <details> element (bg-background-secondary border border-border-primary rounded p-2 mb-2). Summary: 'Show thinking' with cursor-pointer text-sm text-text-secondary. Reasoning (native API field) uses a separate details without background.
goose.tool_call_spacing: Tool calls grouped in div.flex.flex-col.gap-3. Gap of 3 (12px) between consecutive tool calls.
goose.timestamp: text-xs font-mono text-text-secondary. On hover: group-hover:-translate-y-4 group-hover:opacity-0. Copy link appears in absolute position.
goose.consecutive_tools: identifyConsecutiveToolCalls() + shouldHideTimestamp() — timestamps hidden between chains of tool calls, only shown at chain end.
goose.copy_action: MessageCopyLink component shown inline after timestamp slot.

opencode.layout: data-component='assistant-message': display flex, flex-direction column, gap 12px, width 100%. Pure flow layout, no bubble, no background.
opencode.part_system: Messages decomposed into typed parts: text, tool, reasoning, file, agent. Each part type rendered by its own component via PART_MAPPING. This is a fundamentally different architecture — no single 'assistant message' container styles the whole thing.
opencode.assistant_gap: gap: 12px between parts (in session-turn-assistant-content). First text-part has margin-top: 24px.
opencode.thinking: TextShimmer while working, TextReveal for reasoning heading. data-slot='session-turn-thinking' with spinner and font-weight-medium color: var(--text-weak).
opencode.diff_summary: Per-turn file diff summary shown after messages as a sticky collapsible accordion. File paths with directory (truncated rtl) + filename (bold). DiffChanges bars shown inline.
opencode.animation: Spring animation on tool collapsible open/close via motion library: visualDuration 0.35, bounce 0. ShellSubmessage has animated blur+opacity reveal.
opencode.copy_per_part: Copy available per text part, not per message. Copy wrapper: opacity 0 → 1 on hover.

cline.layout: pt-2.5 px-4 wrapper. ChatRow is measured for height via useSize() to trigger scroll-on-height-change. No background on assistant messages.
cline.type_dispatch: ChatRowContent is a massive switch on message.say/ask types. ~30+ cases. Tool call types (bash, file, error, diff, search etc.) each get custom row components.
cline.thinking: ThinkingRow: ml-1 pl-0 mb-0. Summary button text-[13px] with shimmer animation when streaming. Expanded content: max-h-[200px] overflow-y-auto, scroll gradient fade at top/bottom. text-description color. Transition: max-height 250ms cubic-bezier + opacity 150ms.
cline.streaming_indicator: ProgressIndicator (LoaderCircleIcon size-2 animate-spin) inline in header rows while streaming.
cline.accessibility: HEADER_CLASSNAMES constant: flex items-center gap-2.5 mb-3. Consistent row header style.

ava.layout: w-[90%] left-aligned, no background, no border. Similar to Goose.
ava.team_mode: Director messages get pl-3 + border-left: 2px solid var(--amber-4) left bar + DirectorLabel (Crown icon + 'Director' in amber). Unique feature not seen in competitors.
ava.thinking: ThinkingRow below DirectorLabel: <details> with border-left: 2px solid var(--border-subtle), padding-left: 10px. Summary: 11px text-tertiary. Content: 12px opacity-0.7 max-h-300px. Shimmer on summary while streaming.
ava.interleaved_segments: InterleavedThinkingSegments handles thinking blocks interleaved with tool calls — more sophisticated than Goose (which shows thinking above all tools) or Cline (which doesn't interleave).
ava.animation: animate-message-in (when shouldAnimate). animate-fade-in on details element.
ava.timestamps: 10px mono tabular-nums, shows: time · model · tokens · cost · elapsed · mode. Richest metadata of all four tools.
ava.truncation_hint: 'Continue generation' button shown when response appears truncated (no terminal punctuation + >80 chars). Unique quality-of-life feature.
ava.diff_summary: TurnDiffSummary shown after tool calls for completed messages.

components_compared.gap: AVA's timestamp metadata (model, cost, elapsed) is the richest but the 10px size may be too small to read. OpenCode uses a per-turn diff summary with DiffChanges bars — AVA's TurnDiffSummary exists but may lack the inline bar visualization. AVA lacks a per-part copy button (only per-message via MessageActions). OpenCode's 12px gap between assistant parts and 24px margin-top on first text-part creates breathing room AVA may be missing between text/tool segments. Cline's scroll-gradient fade in ThinkingRow is more polished than AVA's plain overflow.
components_compared.fix: 1. Increase timestamp font to 11px minimum for legibility. 2. Add gradient fade at ThinkingRow scroll bottom (AVA uses plain overflow-y-auto). 3. Add inline copy-per-text-segment action for long responses (currently only per-message). 4. Ensure gap between assistant text and tool call segments matches the 12px gap OpenCode uses. Current mb-1/gap-1.5 may be inconsistent.

goose.container: w-full text-sm font-sans rounded-lg overflow-hidden border border-border-primary. When pending approval: border-amber-500/50 bg-amber-50/5.
goose.trigger: Ghost button full-width flex justify-between items-center pr-2. ChevronRight icon (opacity-70, group-hover:opacity-100, rotates 90deg when expanded).
goose.label: ToolIconWithStatus (custom per tool) + getToolLabelContent() which generates human descriptions (e.g., 'reading /path/file', 'running ls'). Very detailed per-tool string building (~650 lines of switch/case).
goose.status_states: LoadingStatus: loading (spinner 8x8 animate-spin), error, success. ToolCallStatus mapped and passed to ToolIconWithStatus.
goose.expandable: Inner ToolDetailsView, ToolLogsView, CodeModeView all use ToolCallExpandable pattern — nested collapsibles with ChevronRight.
goose.logs: ToolLogsView: scrollable log div max-h-[4rem] while loading, max-h-[20rem] when done. Auto-scrolls to bottom on new logs. font-sans text-sm text-textSubtle.
goose.progress: ProgressBar component: determinate (width%) or indeterminate (animate-indeterminate). bg-primary bar on bg-background-subtle track. h-4 rounded-full.
goose.output: Tool result shown in expandable 'Output' section: pl-4 pr-4 py-4. Markdown rendered for text, img for images. Code execution tools get TypeScript syntax highlighting.
goose.approval: Inline amber approval bar: border-t border-amber-500/30. Prompt text in amber-600/amber-400. ToolApprovalButtons below.

opencode.container: BasicTool component. Collapsible wrapper. No explicit border on container — border comes from theme/context.
opencode.trigger: data-component='tool-trigger': flex align-center, gap 0px. Icon (16x16) + structured title/subtitle/args.
opencode.label: title (font-weight-medium text-strong 14px) + subtitle (text-base regular 14px truncated) + args (up to 3, same style as subtitle). label() function checks description/query/url/filePath/path/pattern/name keys.
opencode.shimmer: TextShimmer component on title text when pending/running — animated gradient sweep. Most visually polished loading state of all tools.
opencode.animation: Spring height animation on open/close (motion library, visualDuration 0.35, bounce 0). Much smoother than CSS transitions.
opencode.status: pending/running: blocks collapse (handleOpenChange returns early). locked: prevents collapse (open stays open). Arrow hidden when pending/locked.
opencode.no_explicit_border: The tool trigger has no container border by default — the collapsible context provides styling. Very minimal visual weight when collapsed.

cline.container: Each tool type has its own row component with HEADER_CLASSNAMES. No unified card container — each row type is custom.
cline.header_pattern: HEADER_CLASSNAMES = 'flex items-center gap-2.5 mb-3'. Icon + title text.
cline.thinking_title: ThinkingRow: animate-shimmer on title text (bg-linear-90 from-foreground to-description, bg-[length:200%_100%] bg-clip-text text-transparent). Very polished shimmer gradient.
cline.expand_chevron: ChevronDown/ChevronRight size-1 (tiny!) — much smaller than competitors.
cline.expand_content: max-h-0 → max-h-[200px] + opacity: transition-[max-height] 250ms cubic-bezier + opacity 150ms ease-out. Smooth but cap at 200px.
cline.scroll_gradients: absolute top fade (bg-gradient-to-b from-background to-transparent h-6) + bottom fade visible when canScrollUp/Down. Only tool in analysis with this detail.
cline.streaming_indicator: size-2 animate-spin LoaderCircleIcon inline in header. Very small, subtle.

ava.container: rounded-[var(--radius-md)] border overflow-hidden animate-tool-card-in. Error state: border-[var(--error)]/30. Running: border-[var(--accent)]/30. Default: border-[var(--border-subtle)].
ava.trigger: flex items-center gap-2.5 px-3 py-2 text-[13px] cursor-pointer hover:bg-[var(--alpha-white-3)]. ToolIcon (spinning when running) + summary text (shimmer while running) + elapsed timer + ChevronRight.
ava.shimmer: tool-summary-shimmer CSS class (presumably defined in global CSS). Less declarative than OpenCode's TextShimmer.
ava.timer: Live elapsed counter updating every 1 second via setInterval. Shown as text-[11px] tabular-nums. Switches to completed duration after done. Unique among the four tools.
ava.streaming_output: Live streaming output preview: px-3 pb-2 border-t. pre tag: text-[11px] font-mono max-h-32 overflow-y-auto. Truncated to last 2000 chars.
ava.expand: ChevronRight rotates 90deg. No height animation — content appears immediately. Less polished than OpenCode's spring animation.
ava.accessibility: role='button' tabIndex=0 aria-expanded. Keyboard: Enter/Space toggles. Biome comment explains div+role=button due to WebKitGTK crash with nested button.
ava.delegation_tool: isDelegationTool() check for 'task'/'delegate_*' — renders SubagentCard instead. Context-aware rendering.

components_compared.gap: AVA lacks spring/smooth height animation on tool card expand (OpenCode is best-in-class here with motion library). AVA's shimmer is a CSS class name (tool-summary-shimmer) not a declarative component — less reusable. AVA has no progress bar support. AVA has no nested log view (Goose's ToolLogsView with auto-scroll is very useful for long bash runs). Cline's scroll gradient fade for overflowing content is a polish detail AVA's streaming output section lacks. OpenCode's TextShimmer on title (not just icon) during pending is more prominent than AVA's approach.
components_compared.fix: 1. Add CSS height transition to tool card expand: transition: height 250ms cubic-bezier(0.4, 0, 0.2, 1). Wrap expanded content in measured div or use max-height approach. 2. Replace tool-summary-shimmer CSS class with inline style or a proper Shimmer component for reusability. 3. Add scroll gradient fades to streaming output pre block (absolute positioned gradient overlays top/bottom). 4. Add a ProgressBar sub-component for tools that emit progress events. 5. Consider showing last N lines of streaming output with auto-scroll rather than slicing last 2000 chars.

goose.container: Two separate implementations: (1) reasoningContent: plain <details> mb-2, summary cursor-pointer text-xs text-textSubtle. (2) cotText (chain-of-thought): <details> bg-background-secondary border border-border-primary rounded p-2 mb-2, summary text-sm text-text-secondary.
goose.visual_treatment: CoT thinking gets a background + border card. Reasoning API field gets plain unstyled details. Inconsistency between the two paths.
goose.expand_label: Static: 'Show thinking' / 'Show reasoning' — no timing information.
goose.content_rendering: MarkdownContent — full markdown rendering inside thinking block.
goose.no_streaming_state: No special streaming indicator — just shows whatever content has been received.

opencode.thinking_indicator: While working: TextShimmer on 'Thinking...' text with spinner. data-slot='session-turn-thinking' flex align-center gap-8 color-text-weak font-weight-medium.
opencode.reasoning_heading: TextReveal component (animated reveal) shows extracted heading from reasoning content. Shows alongside shimmer. Smooth fade-in as thinking progresses.
opencode.post_completion: Reasoning parts rendered inline in assistant-content area with showReasoningSummaries control. No explicit collapsible — part system handles it.
opencode.no_separate_card: No dedicated thinking card UI — thinking is integrated into the part rendering pipeline.

cline.container: ml-1 pl-0 mb-0 -mt-[2px]. Title button: inline-flex gap-0.5 text-description text-[13px].
cline.shimmer: animate-shimmer bg-linear-90 from-foreground to-description bg-[length:200%_100%] bg-clip-text text-transparent when isStreaming. Gradient runs across text. Best text shimmer of all four.
cline.expanded_content: max-h-0 → max-h-[200px]. transition-[max-height] 250ms cubic-bezier(0.4,0,0.2,1) + opacity 150ms ease-out. text-description text-sm scrollbar-none [direction:ltr].
cline.scroll_gradients: from-background gradient fade at top (when canScrollUp) and bottom (when canScrollDown). 6px pointer-events-none gradient overlays.
cline.no_timing: No 'Thought for Ns' — just the title prop passed in.

ava.container: <details> mb-2 animate-fade-in. border-left: 2px solid var(--border-subtle). padding-left: 10px.
ava.summary: cursor-pointer 11px text-tertiary no list-style. flex items-center gap-4px. Shimmer class (thinking-shimmer) when streaming.
ava.timing: 'Thinking...' while streaming, 'Thought for Ns' or 'Thought' when done. Duration tracked from component mount. Unique among four — Goose doesn't show timing, Cline doesn't, OpenCode shows shimmer but no timing.
ava.content: mt-1 max-h-[300px] overflow-y-auto scrollbar-thin. 12px opacity-0.7 text-tertiary. Full markdown rendering via renderMarkdown/renderMarkdownStreaming.
ava.streaming_cursor: ▍ block cursor at end of content during streaming.
ava.throttled_render: 150ms throttle on markdown re-render during streaming to avoid thrashing.

components_compared.gap: AVA's scroll gradient fade is missing (Cline has it). AVA's thinking block renders below the director label and above plan cards — correct hierarchy. AVA's summary text shimmer (thinking-shimmer CSS class) should be as declarative as Cline's inline animate-shimmer + bg-clip-text gradient which is visually superior. The 300px max-height cap could be user-configurable. AVA shows timing (Thought for Ns) — this is a differentiating feature that should be preserved and made more prominent.
components_compared.fix: 1. Add scroll gradient fades to thinking content div: after:absolute after:bottom-0 after:left-0 after:right-0 after:h-8 after:bg-gradient-to-t after:from-[var(--background)] after:to-transparent after:pointer-events-none. 2. Upgrade thinking-shimmer CSS to use bg-clip-text gradient approach similar to Cline for a more premium feel. 3. Surface the 'Thought for Ns' timing more prominently — e.g., 11px semi-bold instead of plain text.

goose.rendering: MarkdownContent component wraps all text. No explicit code block wrapper details visible from component code — relies on MarkdownContent CSS.
goose.tool_code: Code execution args shown in MarkdownContent wrapped in triple-backtick typescript fence.
goose.output_section: Tool result text in MarkdownContent with whitespace-pre-wrap overflow-x-auto.

opencode.markdown: Separate markdown.css and markdown.tsx component. Not read in this analysis but referenced by message-part.
opencode.syntax_highlighting: Inferred from tool arg display — file icon per file type, diff-changes bars. Likely Shiki or similar.
opencode.diff_view: DiffChanges component with 'bars' variant for summary and full diff view in session-turn-diff-view. Scrollable, overflow-x hidden.

cline.code_accordian: CodeAccordian component imported in ChatRow. cleanPathPrefix utility. Collapsible code blocks.
cline.diff_display: DiffEditRow component for file edits. Likely has inline diff rendering.
cline.shell_output: CommandOutputRow with CommandOutputContent — specialized bash output rendering.

ava.rendering: markdown-it based rendering (renderMarkdown/renderMarkdownStreaming from lib/markdown.ts). DOMPurify sanitization. code-block-wrapper CSS class wrapping all code blocks.
ava.copy_button: data-copy-code button injected into wrapper. Copy → clipboard. Checkmark animation on success (2s). SVG icon swap.
ava.apply_button: data-apply-code button for writing code to file path. Dispatches ava:apply-code event. Applied! feedback.
ava.expand_collapse: code-collapsed CSS class + 'Show all (N lines)' button for blocks >20 lines. 'Show less' to collapse again.
ava.line_numbers: data-line-numbers attribute on container — CSS-driven via settings().behavior.lineNumbers.
ava.language_label: Inferred from markdown rendering — likely shows language in code block header.

components_compared.gap: AVA's code block expand/collapse (20-line threshold, Show all button) is a good UX feature that Goose and Cline lack. AVA's apply-to-file feature is also unique. Missing compared to OpenCode: per-file-type icons in file references. Missing compared to Goose: explicit code execution output section with TypeScript syntax awareness. The code-block-wrapper classes need verification that they render consistently across themes.
components_compared.fix: 1. Add language label to code block header (e.g., 'typescript' displayed in header). 2. Add line number column display when data-line-numbers is active (verify CSS exists). 3. Consider showing a collapsible diff view within tool calls that produce diffs (TurnDiffSummary likely covers this already).

goose.loading: isStreamingMessage prop passed to ToolCallWithResponse. LoadingDot animation on tool cards (status 'loading'). No explicit typing indicator for pre-content state.
goose.tool_status: LoadingStatus mapping: !toolResponse + !isStreamingComplete = loading dot. Smooth transition to success/error.

opencode.working_state: TextShimmer on 'Thinking...' text + spinner. showThinking() computed signal shows this only when no visible parts yet.
opencode.auto_scroll: createAutoScroll hook with overflowAnchor: 'dynamic'. Handles user scroll interaction to pause auto-scroll. Most sophisticated scroll management of all four.
opencode.session_retry: SessionRetry component shows retry status inline.
opencode.tool_pending: TextShimmer on tool title during pending/running state.

cline.spinner: LoaderCircleIcon size-2 animate-spin shown inline in headers. Very small.
cline.thinking_stream: ThinkingRow renders content as it arrives, auto-scrolls to bottom via scrollRef.current.scrollTop = scrollHeight.
cline.progress_indicator: ProgressIndicator = <LoaderCircleIcon className='size-2 mr-2 animate-spin' />. Used in multiple row types.

ava.live_block: LiveStreamingBlock component shown while agent is running — separate from MessageBubble. Renders above all completed messages.
ava.typing_indicator: Three typing-dot spans with animation-delay staggering (0/160/320ms) shown when no content/thinking/tools yet. 'ava is thinking...' text in font-ui-mono.
ava.interleaved: Full interleaved thinking+tools rendering during live stream — most sophisticated of all four.
ava.streaming_cursor: ▍ block cursor in both MarkdownContent and ThinkingRow during streaming.
ava.content_refresh: 150ms throttle prevents markdown re-renders faster than necessary.
ava.fade_in: animate-fade-in on outer container. density-py for spacing.

components_compared.gap: AVA's typing indicator (three dots) appears before any content and disappears once content starts — good pattern. However the 'ava is thinking...' text label may be redundant with the dots. OpenCode's auto-scroll hook (overflowAnchor dynamic + user-interaction pause) is more robust than whatever AVA uses. AVA's LiveStreamingBlock is architecturally separate from MessageBubble — verify there's no visual layout jump when streaming ends and the block converts to a stored message.
components_compared.fix: 1. Verify smooth transition from LiveStreamingBlock to stored MessageBubble on completion — should be seamless. 2. Consider adopting OpenCode's overflowAnchor dynamic approach for scroll management. 3. The typing indicator text 'ava is thinking...' could be styled more subtly — smaller font, or omit entirely and rely on dots.

goose.trigger: Edit button revealed on hover (opacity-0 → group-hover:opacity-100, -translate-y-4 → translate-y-0). Icon + 'Edit' text.
goose.edit_ui: Full-width centered overlay (max-w-4xl mx-auto). border rounded-xl shadow-lg py-4 px-4. Textarea: min-h-120px max-h-300px, focus:ring-2 ring-blue-400. Auto-resizes to scrollHeight capped at 200px.
goose.actions: Cancel (ghost) + 'Edit in Place' (secondary) + 'Fork Session' (primary). Helper text explains difference.
goose.error_handling: Inline error message with role='alert' aria-live='polite' for empty message validation.

opencode.edit_ui: Not directly observed in session-turn.tsx. Likely handled via dock-prompt or inline-input components.
opencode.notes: Edit functionality exists but implementation not examined in scope.

cline.trigger: Click anywhere on user bubble to enter edit mode (entire bubble is clickable).
cline.edit_ui: DynamicTextArea (react-textarea-autosize) replacing bubble text. VS Code theme variables. resize: none, overflowY: scroll, scrollbarWidth: none.
cline.actions: 'Restore All' + 'Restore Chat' — checkpoint-linked. These persist conversation state.
cline.keyboard: Enter = Restore Chat, Cmd+Enter = Restore All, Escape = cancel.
cline.blur_handling: Custom blur handler checks if focus moves to action buttons before closing — prevents accidental close.

ava.trigger: onStartEdit from MessageActions (revealed on hover via group-hover pattern).
ava.edit_ui: EditForm component (separate file). initialContent prop, onSave/onCancel callbacks.
ava.notes: EditForm not read in this analysis but referenced in MessageBubble.

components_compared.gap: AVA's EditForm needs review to confirm it matches the quality of Goose's implementation (full-width overlay, auto-resize, keyboard shortcuts). Cline's checkpoint-linked edit (Restore All / Restore Chat) is a unique feature AVA should consider — it maps to AVA's /rewind command.
components_compared.fix: 1. Verify EditForm has: auto-resize textarea, Escape=cancel, Cmd+Enter=save, empty validation with accessible error. 2. Consider adding 'Rewind to here' option to user message edit UI, linking to AVA's checkpoint/rewind feature. 3. If EditForm uses a fixed height, convert to auto-resize with min/max.

css_recommendations: Add --chat-user-bg and --chat-user-text semantic tokens explicitly in tokens.css with distinct values for each theme. Current fallback is unclear. Minimum contrast ratio 4.5:1 against background.
Cap user message width at min(85%, 68ch) to prevent excessively long lines: change max-w-[85%] to something like style={{ maxWidth: 'min(85%, 68ch)' }}

Add CSS height animation to ToolCallCard expand/collapse using max-height approach: .tool-expand-content { max-height: 0; overflow: hidden; transition: max-height 250ms cubic-bezier(0.4, 0, 0.2, 1); } .tool-expand-content.open { max-height: 2000px; }
Add scroll gradient fades to ThinkingRow content area and ToolCallCard streaming output area using pseudo-elements or absolute positioned divs with gradient backgrounds.

Upgrade tool-summary-shimmer from CSS class to inline computed style using bg-gradient approach: background: linear-gradient(90deg, var(--text-secondary) 0%, var(--text-primary) 50%, var(--text-secondary) 100%); background-size: 200% 100%; animation: shimmer 1.5s infinite; -webkit-background-clip: text; color: transparent.
Add .thinking-content scrollbar-gutter: stable to prevent layout shift when thinking content scrollbar appears/disappears.
Increase timestamp metadata font from 10px to 11px minimum. 10px is below typical legibility threshold on non-retina displays.
Add gap between consecutive tool calls in the message view: the current gap-1.5 (6px) may be too tight for readability. Use gap-2 (8px) minimum.
Ensure the animate-tool-card-in animation duration is ≤150ms to avoid perceived jank on rapid tool streaming. Verify keyframe definition.
Add will-change: opacity to timestamp hover transitions to hint compositor for GPU-accelerated fade.

Consider a tool-card-error-bg token: currently error state uses border-[var(--error)]/30 only. Add a very subtle background tint: background: rgba(239, 68, 68, 0.04) to make error cards visually distinct without being alarming.
priority_fixes: 1. TOOL CARD EXPAND ANIMATION: Add smooth height transition to ToolCallCard content expand/collapse. Currently content pops in immediately with no animation. OpenCode's spring animation (motion library) and Cline's 250ms cubic-bezier are both significantly more polished. Minimum viable: add CSS max-height transition. Files: src/components/chat/ToolCallCard.tsx + global CSS.

2. SCROLL GRADIENT FADES IN OVERFLOW CONTAINERS: Add top/bottom gradient fades to any scrollable text container (ThinkingRow content div, ToolCallCard streaming output pre). This single detail elevates perceived polish significantly. Only Cline implements this currently. Files: src/components/chat/message-rows/ThinkingRow.tsx, src/components/chat/ToolCallCard.tsx.

3. USER BUBBLE CONTRAST AND WIDTH CAP: Verify --chat-user-bg has sufficient contrast in all 29 themes. Add 68ch width cap to user messages. The bubble is the primary user-facing visual element and its visual weight needs to be consistently strong. Files: src/styles/tokens.css, src/components/chat/MessageBubble.tsx.

4. THINKING SHIMMER UPGRADE: Replace thinking-shimmer CSS class with an inline bg-clip-text gradient shimmer matching Cline's animate-shimmer technique. The shimmer is the primary visual cue that the model is reasoning — it should be prominent and premium. Files: src/components/chat/message-rows/ThinkingRow.tsx + global CSS.

5. TIMESTAMP LEGIBILITY: Increase base timestamp font from 10px to 11px. Consider making the model name bold or semi-bold within the metadata line to create visual hierarchy in the metadata string (e.g., time · **claude-sonnet-4** · 1,234 tokens · $0.003 · 4.2s). Files: src/components/chat/MessageBubble.tsx.

rendering_model_comparison.goose: Message-level components (GooseMessage/UserMessage). Tools attached to message via toolRequests array. Single-level architecture.
rendering_model_comparison.opencode: Part-based decomposition. Messages are collections of typed parts (text/tool/reasoning/file/agent). Most flexible and scalable. SessionTurn is a turn-level container that orchestrates all parts.
rendering_model_comparison.cline: Type-dispatch architecture. Single ChatRow switch on message.say/ask type generates specialized rows. ~30 types. Most complex single-component but easy to extend individual message types.
rendering_model_comparison.ava: Segment-based with interleaved thinking. MessageBubble segments message content into text+tool interleaved groups. InterleavedThinkingSegments handles thinking models. Most sophisticated thinking integration. Architecture is between Goose (simpler) and OpenCode (more decomposed).

ava_unique_strengths: Interleaved thinking+tool rendering (thinking blocks matched to the tool calls that followed them) — no competitor does this as cleanly
Live elapsed timer on tool cards (updates every 1 second during execution)
Asymmetric chat bubble corners (rounded-2xl / rounded-br-sm) for user messages — true chat bubble aesthetic
Timestamp metadata richness (time + model + tokens + cost + elapsed + mode)
Truncation detection heuristic with 'Continue generation' affordance
Director amber left-border + crown label in team mode
Team domain color coding for lead questions (LeadQuestionCard)
ava_gaps_vs_competitors: No smooth height animation on tool card expand (OpenCode spring animation, Cline cubic-bezier)
No scroll gradient fades in overflowing text containers (Cline has this)
tool-summary-shimmer is less declarative/reusable than TextShimmer component
No per-segment copy button (OpenCode has per-part copy)
No progress bar for long-running tools
User bubble max-width has no char-count cap (OpenCode: min(82%, 64ch))
ThinkingRow uses native <details> which has limited animation capability vs Cline's custom collapsible with max-height transitions
